text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers review each other's code before it is merged, they can catch bugs early, which saves time and money. Beyond finding mistakes, code reviews encourage knowledge sharing within the team. Junior developers learn from experienced colleagues, and everyone gains a better understanding of the project as a whole. This process also improves overall team quality. By discussing different solutions and asking questions, the team establishes better coding standards and writes cleaner code. In short, code reviews help build stronger, more reliable software and create a culture of collaboration and growth.
